observations.correspondence_rationale: The passage describes speech that is not understood by others, an instrument shattered, and a tongue made heavy, speaking a foretold truth heard only as noise or babble of the judged. This closely parallels Isaiah 28:11-12, where God speaks through foreign tongues as a sign and wonder to the people, symbolizing judgment and incomprehensible speech.
observations.summary: The text explicitly alludes to Isaiah 28:11-12 by depicting a scenario where speech is rendered unintelligible and burdensome, symbolizing divine judgment and the futility of communication to the judged.

evidence.quote: When you speak, no man shall understand. Your instrument shall be shattered. Your tongue shall be made a heavy thing, speaking a foretold truth that is only ever heard as noise—the worthless babble of the judged.
